originalTitle: Adobe CFO Quits to Join a Chipmaker. You Shouldn’t Quit ADBE Stock.
summary: Adobe stock has fallen to a seven-year low, and the AI disruption fears are being compounded by the departure of its top leadership. Is ADBE stock a buy at these levels?
content:
All information and data in this article is solely for informational purposes. For more information please view the Barchart Disclosure Policy here

The business models of legacy software and information technology (IT) companies are being threatened by artificial intelligence (AI) . It's not only investors who are ditching these companies to chase hot AI stocks, but also even top executives are on a similar path.

During its fiscal Q2 2026 earnings call, Adobe (ADBE) announced that its CFO, Dan Durn, would depart the company. Durn would join Marvell Technologies (MRVL) , whose shares have more than tripled this year. Adobe, on the other hand, is trading near multi-year lows and is among the top losers of the S&P 500 Index ($SPX) in 2026.

A cursory look at the index constituents underscores AI being the predominant theme this year. Accenture (ACN) , Intuit (INTU) , Gartner (IT) , Workday, Salesforce (CRM) , and Cognizant Technology (CTSH) are among the worst-performing S&P 500 Index stocks this year, with all losing at least a third of their market cap. These names are perceived as AI losers and have been squarely out of favor with investors .

Stacked at the top are AI companies, in particular chip and memory plays like SanDisk (SNDK) , Micron (MU) , Intel (INTC) , and Seagate (STX) . Notably, Intel, which fell to multi-year lows last year , has been an investor favorite and has risen sixfold from its 2025 lows.

Coming back to Adobe, it previously announced the departure of long-time CEO Shantanu Narayan during the Q1 earnings call in March. Losing both the CEO and CFO in a span of three months is hardly a positive development for any company and has only added fuel to ADBE’s sell-off.

Meanwhile, Adobe’s fiscal Q2 earnings were better-than-expected, with revenues rising 13% year-over-year (YOY) to a record $6.62 billion. The adjusted earnings per share (EPS) came in at $5.96, which easily beat the $5.83 that analysts were modelling. In the current quarter, Adobe expects to post revenues between $6.67 billion and $6.72 billion, which is also ahead of estimates. The company raised its annual revenue guidance to between $26.5 billion and $26.6 billion while projecting adjusted EPS between $24.35 and $24.45. However, the earnings beat and upbeat guidance were more than overshadowed by the announcement of the CFO’s departure , and the stock is trading sharply lower this past Thursday and Friday, June 11 and 12.

In my previous article, I noted that while I am keeping ADBE on my watchlist, I did not find the stock a compelling buy yet. With Adobe now falling to a seven-year low, let’s revisit the stock’s forecast.

Adobe is currently in a leadership transition phase and is headhunting for both a CEO and a CFO. While leadership transitions add a layer of uncertainty and jitteriness among investors, I believe Adobe stock looks like a buy at these levels. Here’s my bullish thesis for the embattled stock.

Of 38 analysts, 23 of the analysts rate ADBE as a “Hold.” Ten analysts rank it as a “Buy," with one rating it as a “Moderate Buy.” Four outliers are bearish providing a “Strong Sell.” However, in what seems a reflection of its deep value status, ADBE even trades below the Street-low target price of $190, while the mean target price of $313.23 implies 53.5% upside from here. The Street-high price point of $460 points to an upside of 125.5% for the following twelve months.

Overall, with AI now showing signs of a bubble , perceived “AI losers” like Adobe could be good bets. The stock may be a good hedge against an AI-led sell-off, perhaps regaining favor with markets. The near-term trigger for the stock might be the appointment of a new leadership team, which could possibly formulate a strategic roadmap to revive investor confidence.
chineseTitle: Adobe 首席财务官辞职加入一家芯片制造商。你不应该退出 ADBE 股票。
chineseSummary: Adobe 股价已跌至七年来的最低点，而其最高领导层的离职加剧了人们对人工智能颠覆的担忧。 ADBE 股票在这些水平上值得买入吗？
chineseContent:
本文中的所有信息和数据仅供参考。欲了解更多信息，请查看此处的条形图披露政策

传统软件和信息技术 (IT) 公司的商业模式正受到人工智能 (AI) 的威胁。不仅投资者抛弃这些公司，转而追逐热门的人工智能股票，就连高管也走上了类似的道路。

在 2026 年第二财季财报电话会议上，Adobe (ADBE) 宣布其首席财务官 Dan Durn 将离开公司。 Durn 将加入 Marvell Technologies (MRVL)，该公司的股价今年已增加两倍多。另一方面，Adobe 的交易价格接近多年低点，是 2026 年标准普尔 500 指数 ($SPX) 跌幅最大的公司之一。

粗略地看一下指数成分股就可以看出，人工智能是今年的主要主题。埃森哲 (ACN)、Intuit (INTU)、Gartner (IT)、Workday、Salesforce (CRM) 和 Cognizant Technology (CTSH) 是今年表现最差的标普 500 指数股票，全部市值都损失了至少三分之一。这些名字被认为是人工智能的失败者，并且完全不受投资者的青睐。

位居榜首的是人工智能公司，特别是芯片和内存公司，如 SanDisk (SNDK)、Micron (MU)、Intel (INTC) 和 Seagate (STX)。值得注意的是，去年跌至多年低点的英特尔一直是投资者的最爱，并且已从 2025 年的低点上涨了六倍。

回到 Adob​​e，该公司此前在 3 月份的第一季度财报电话会议上宣布长期担任首席执行官的 Shantanu Narayan 离职。在三个月内失去首席执行官和首席财务官对任何公司来说都不是一个积极的发展，只会加剧 ADBE 的抛售。

与此同时，Adobe 第二季度财报好于预期，收入同比增长 13%，达到创纪录的 66.2 亿美元。调整后每股收益 (EPS) 为 5.96 美元，轻松超过分析师预测的 5.83 美元。 Adobe 预计本季度营收将在 66.7 亿美元至 67.2 亿美元之间，这也超出了预期。该公司将年度收入指导上调至 265 亿美元至 266 亿美元之间，同时预计调整后每股收益在 24.35 美元至 24.45 美元之间。然而，首席财务官离职的消息掩盖了盈利预期和乐观指引，该股在上周四和周五（6 月 11 日和 12 日）大幅下跌。

在我之前的文章中，我指出，虽然我将 ADBE 保留在我的观察名单上，但我尚未发现该股票值得买入。随着 Adob​​e 现在跌至七年来的低点，让我们重新审视该股的预测。

Adobe 目前正处于领导层过渡阶段，正在物色首席执行官和首席财务官。虽然领导层换届给投资者带来了一层不确定性和不安，但我认为 Adob​​e 股票在目前的水平上看起来值得买入。这是我对这只陷入困境的股票的看涨论点。

在 38 名分析师中，23 名分析师将 ADBE 评级为“持有”。十位分析师将其评级为“买入”，其中一位分析师将其评级为“适度买入”。四个异常值是看跌的，提供“强力卖出”。然而，ADBE 的交易价格甚至低于 190 美元的市场最低目标价，而 313.23 美元的平均目标价意味着较此价格有 53.5% 的上涨空间，这似乎反映了其深层价值状态。 460 美元的市场最高价预示着未来 12 个月的上涨空间为 125.5%。

总体而言，随着人工智能现在显示出泡沫迹象，像 Adob​​e 这样被认为是“人工智能失败者”的公司可能是不错的选择。该股可能是抵御人工智能引发的抛售的良好对冲工具，或许会重新受到市场青睐。该股的近期触发因素可能是新领导团队的任命，该团队可能会制定战略路线图以重振投资者信心。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
